page: Amazon Web Services r5d.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Additionally, it includes 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing and memory subsystem performance. The instance achieved a strong rating in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and passmark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth is a notable strength, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 4504.62 GB/sec and large block uncached write bandwidth at 98.64 GB/sec. Database operations are highly efficient, as demonstrated by a strong passmark database score of 21671.55 and 77.77 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.

While multi-threaded and memory-bound tasks perform well, single-threaded operations show moderate results, with gzip compression rated as weak. LLM text generation is a significant bottleneck, with text generation on a medium model scoring in the bottom 10% at 5.49 tokens per second, reflecting the lack of dedicated GPU hardware. Given the high memory capacity, local NVMe storage, and strong database benchmarks, this instance is suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and memory-intensive data processing workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services r5d.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical Intel Xeon 8259CL cores running at 3.1 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the Nitro hypervisor with a 25 Gbps baseline network. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent database execution, with Redis SET operations reaching 77.77 million operations per second. However, single-threaded compression and LLM text generation performance are relatively weak due to the lack of hardware accelerators. This instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads, large databases, and high-throughput caching applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5d.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core CPU and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5d.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 3600 GB NVMe SSD storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized server combining high-capacity DDR4 RAM with local NVMe SSD storage for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical Intel Xeon cores at 3.1 GHz
768 GB of DDR4 memory with strong read and write bandwidth
3600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
Strong multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Well-suited for large databases, Redis caching, and memory-intensive applications
